metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `monitoring_merge_change.tf` (or extend an existing monitoring tf) declaring a `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` for `rebar/host:review_bot_merge_change_errors` wired to `aws_sns_topic.alerts`, matching the pattern in `monitoring_s4b.tf` / `monitoring_1fa8.tf`, and record it in S2's explicit edit targets.
findings.finding: The new `review_bot_merge_change_errors` CloudWatch metric (published via observability.sh) has no corresponding CloudWatch alarm Terraform resource, breaking the established IaC pattern for all peer error-class metrics.
findings.location: S2 AC — 'published as a CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` (reason-tagged) via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` exactly as `voter_errors` is, plus a "signals to watch" entry in the runbook'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/terraform/monitoring_merge_change.tf` mirroring `monitoring_1fa8.tf`: namespace=`rebar/host`, metric_name=`review_bot_merge_change_errors`, statistic=`Sum`, period=300, evaluation_periods=1, threshold=0, comparison_operator=`GreaterThanThreshold`, treat_missing_data=`notBreaching`, alarm_actions=`[aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]`. Update S2's edit-targets list to include this file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3, either (a) pre-specify the exact `gerrit_to_platform.ini` stanza format for `feature/*` branch dispatch based on g2p docs/source NOW and include it in the AC, or (b) explicitly state that if config is needed the executor should add a `[branch "refs/heads/feature/*"]` entry mirroring the existing `[branch "refs/heads/main"]` block — so the executor has a concrete template rather than an open-ended investigation.
findings.finding: S3's done-definition defers the g2p config design choice to the executor: it says 'determine from g2p source/docs whether gerrit_to_platform.ini filters dispatch by branch' and offers two branches ('either the confirmed required feature/* config, or a confirmed no-config-needed no-op') without specifying the ini stanza format/keys if config IS required. An executor who discovers that config IS needed has no spec for what to write.
findings.location: S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes (AC bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the S3 AC: 'If g2p requires per-branch config, the required stanza mirrors the existing main branch entry with `branch = refs/heads/feature/*`; the ini diff is committed to `infra/` and reviewed as part of this story.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend S6's back-out documentation to cover the mid-flight rollback scenario: specify the procedure for disposing of an in-flight feature branch (e.g., abandon any open merge change via Gerrit UI/REST, preserve the branch ref for post-incident recovery, then delete or keep per team policy), and add this as a named sub-case in the runbook's back-out section.
findings.finding: The S6 back-out procedure covers only the clean-slate case (no active feature branch). It does not address the mid-flight rollback scenario where a feature branch already has committed story changes and/or an open merge change in Gerrit: revoking pushMerge + feature/* ACLs in that state leaves the branch stranded (cannot submit the merge change, cannot push further changes), and no procedure is given for how to dispose of the stranded branch or in-flight merge change (admin git-ref deletion, abandon + admin cleanup, etc.).
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out; 'back-out documented + tested (revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'mid-flight rollback' sub-section to the S6 back-out doc: (1) abandon any open merge change targeting main (Gerrit REST: POST /changes/{id}/abandon); (2) leave the feature branch ref intact for post-incident recovery (all story history is safe in Gerrit); (3) after the incident, an admin can either re-grant ACLs to complete the merge or cherry-pick story commits individually; (4) document the admin git-ref-deletion command for final cleanup once the situation is resolved.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add group-creation logic to `setup-project.sh` before the `want`-dict resolution step: call `gerrit create-group feature-branch-drivers` (or `POST /a/groups/feature-branch-drivers` via REST) idempotently (tolerate 'already exists'), then add the name to the `want` dict — so the script truly creates-or-resolves the group without a manual prerequisite.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that extending `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict will 'create/resolve' the `feature-branch-drivers` group declaratively, but the existing `want`-dict mechanism in `setup-project.sh` (lines 57–64) only RESOLVES a group's UUID via `gerrit ls-groups --verbose` — it has no `gerrit create-group` call and cannot create a group that does not already exist in Gerrit. If `feature-branch-drivers` is absent, `ls-groups` won't return it, its UUID won't be written to the `groups` file, and the `git push` to `refs/meta/config` will be rejected by Gerrit with 'group not found'. The plan's 'created/resolved declaratively' claim conflates two distinct operations that the current script only half-implements.
findings.location: S1 AC — 'extending setup-project.sh's managed-groups want dict (~line 57, currently only Administrators + Service Users) so the group is created/resolved declaratively'; infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh lines 57–64
findings.suggested_fix: Insert before the `declare -A want=…` block: `$GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group 'feature-branch-drivers' 2>&1 | grep -vE 'already exists' || true`. Then add `["feature-branch-drivers"]=1` to the `want` dict. This makes the script idempotent: it creates if absent, skips if present, and always resolves the UUID.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S2: Specify the journald grep pattern for the new `review_bot_merge_change_errors` observability.sh section — either define the pattern string (e.g. `merge_change_409_guard|mergelist_fetch_error`) explicitly, or document that `merge_change_409_guard`/`mergelist_fetch_error` events must ALSO emit the existing `VOTER_ERROR` marker so the existing probe section catches them without a new section.
findings.finding: The plan says to publish `review_bot_merge_change_errors` via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` 'exactly as `voter_errors` is,' but the live observability.sh publishes `voter_errors` by grep-counting the literal string `VOTER_ERROR` in journald. The plan's new structured log events are named `merge_change_409_guard` and `mergelist_fetch_error` — neither matches `VOTER_ERROR`. The plan never specifies what journald grep pattern the new observability.sh section will use for the new metric, nor whether these new events should also emit the `VOTER_ERROR` marker to share the existing probe section.
findings.location: S2 — Observability sub-section: 'published as a CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` … via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` exactly as `voter_errors` is'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the S2 observability spec: 'the `merge_change_409_guard` and `mergelist_fetch_error` log events MUST also emit the `VOTER_ERROR` journald marker (same as `_voter_error()` does today) so the existing observability.sh §4 grep counts them automatically; the new `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric is published by a new §4c section in observability.sh using `grep -cE "merge_change_409_guard|mergelist_fetch_error"` against the same voter container journald.'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `assemble_merge_change_context`'s output is shaped to survive `compose_diff_context`'s unconditional ` ```diff ` wrapping — either (a) define the merge context format to be valid diff-fenceable content (auto-merge diff first, mergelist appended as a diff comment block), or (b) add a new `assemble_merge_change_context` entry-point in `produce_code_review_verdict`/`assemble_diff_context` that bypasses the diff-fence wrapping and produces the top-level `## Merge context` / `## Auto-merge diff` sections directly in the kernel's `context` string.
findings.finding: The assembled merge-change context string (commit subjects + auto-merge diff) is threaded as `diff_text` through the existing seam into `produce_code_review_verdict` → `assemble_diff_context` → `compose_diff_context`, which unconditionally wraps the entire string in a ` ```diff ` code fence (assemble.py:88-93). The `## Merge context` section (plain-text commit subject lines) will be presented to the LLM's finder and verify agents inside a diff code fence — a format mismatch that the plan neither acknowledges nor mitigates. For the clean-merge case (empty auto-merge diff), the entire LLM prompt's `## Diff` section contains only narrative text inside a diff fence, with no actual diff content, which may impair the LLM's ability to reason about the change correctly.
findings.location: S2 — 'Data flow into the gate is unchanged: the assembled string is passed as `diff_text` through the existing seam'; src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py:77-93 (`compose_diff_context`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S2: Specify the exact call-site for the `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` count interpolation — e.g. 'add an optional `suffix_extra: str = ""` parameter to `_message_tag` so the merge path calls `_message_tag(reason, suffix_extra=f" (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)")` without modifying `_TAG_SUFFIXES` or breaking existing callers.'
findings.finding: The plan says the robot comment for merge changes reuses 'the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` verdict keys, appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the existing suffix text.' The live `_TAG_SUFFIXES` is a dict of fixed literal strings (e.g. `'PASS': 'PASS'`, `'finding': 'BLOCK — finding'`) used by `_message_tag(reason)` which does a direct dict lookup and string interpolation. The plan never specifies WHERE in the call stack the dynamic integer `N` is interpolated — whether `_message_tag` gains a new parameter, a wrapper is called instead, or the full tag is built at the voter call site — leaving the mutation point ambiguous for an executor who must not break the existing tested vocabulary.
findings.location: S2 — 'the merge-change variant REUSES the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` verdict keys in `adapter.py`, appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the existing suffix text … e.g. `[LLM-Review: PASS (merge-change, 12 integrated commits)]`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `suffix_extra: str = ''` to `_message_tag`; the merge path passes `suffix_extra=f' (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)'`; all existing callers pass nothing and are unchanged; the test that asserts `_TAG_SUFFIXES` vocabulary is unmodified continues to pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `DiffInfo`→unified-diff transformation in `get_file_diff`: either (a) use `?intraline=false&whitespace=IGNORE_NONE&context=ALL_LINES` and reconstruct unified-diff from DiffInfo's `content` hunks (document the format produced), or (b) switch to an endpoint that already returns unified-diff text (e.g., `revisions/{rev}/patch?parent=2` is broken per R1, so option (a) is correct); add a unit test that asserts `get_file_diff` output is a valid unified-diff string (starts with `diff --git` or `---`/`+++` headers).
findings.finding: The plan specifies `get_file_diff` (no parent) to fetch per-file diffs against the auto-merge base, but the Gerrit REST endpoint `GET revisions/{rev}/files/{file-id}/diff` returns a structured `DiffInfo` JSON object (with `meta_a`, `meta_b`, `content` hunks array, etc.) — NOT a unified-diff text string. The plan's `assemble_merge_change_context` contract shows the result being assembled as `## Auto-merge diff` content passed directly as `diff_text` through the existing seam, but the seam and the LLM gate (`produce_code_review_verdict(diff_text=...)`) expect unified-diff format text. No transformation from `DiffInfo` JSON to unified-diff text is specified anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: S2 AC — `gerrit_client.py` new `get_file_diff` method; `assemble_merge_change_context` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`
findings.suggested_fix: In `gerrit_client.get_file_diff`, after parsing the DiffInfo JSON response, reconstruct a unified-diff string: emit `--- a/{path}` / `+++ b/{path}` headers and rebuild `@@`-hunk lines from `content[].ab`/`.b`/`.a` arrays. Alternatively, use the `format=TEXT` query param if Gerrit 3.14.1 supports it on the diff endpoint (verify against the running instance as part of S2 fixture capture). Document the chosen approach and format in the method docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S6 back-out AC must include: 'remove or disable the aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm Terraform resource added by S2 if rolling back fully; confirm Terraform plan is clean post-rollback.'
findings.finding: S2 introduces a new Terraform resource (`aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` modelled on `monitoring_s4b.tf`) for `review_bot_merge_change_errors`, but no sibling child — including S6 (back-out) — mentions this alarm in back-out procedures, and no child specifies where this Terraform resource lives or which apply/plan CI step manages it. The parent S2 criterion mentions the CloudWatch metric via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` but does not mention a Terraform alarm resource, so this is S2-only scope that has no back-out coverage.
findings.location: child 29a6-59d1-a3ba-40ee (S2) — Observability bullet; child 72b8-4cda-ccd4-414d (S6) — Back-out AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S6's back-out section: 'Terraform: remove the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` alarm resource introduced in S2 (or leave in place if the metric emission is also rolled back via observability.sh revert) and run terraform plan to confirm no drift.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either move the `Push Merge Commit` ACL grant from S1 into S2 (so the bot fix and the ACL go live together), or add an explicit note in S1 that the pushMerge ACL line should be committed to the repo in S1 but applied to the live Gerrit server only after S2 ships.
findings.finding: The pushMerge ACL is granted as part of S1 (which completes before S2 begins), but the bot's 409 fail-closed bug on merge commits is only fixed in S2. The sequencing section says 'S2 MUST precede any real merge change' but does not split the pushMerge ACL grant out of S1 to defer it until after S2 ships. An implementer following the AC as written would enable pushMerge in S1, leaving a window where the ACL is live but the bot would fail-closed -1 on any merge change attempted before S2 completes.
findings.location: Sequencing & risk section + S1 AC + S2 AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-step to S1: 'Commit the pushMerge ACL line to infra/gerrit/project.config in this story but annotate setup-project.sh to skip applying it until S2 is deployed (or apply it as the final step of S2 cut-over).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to S6's back-out section an explicit step: record the current LLM-Review `copyCondition` value before S1 applies the change (analogous to recording the submit-type prior value), and document restoring it to `changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` as a back-out step alongside the ACL and submit-type reversions.
findings.finding: S6's back-out inventory omits reverting the LLM-Review label `copyCondition` change (adding `changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`). If the new copyCondition causes unexpected vote-carry behavior on existing changes, operators following S6 have no documented step to restore the prior copyCondition value.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out / Acceptance Criteria S6
findings.suggested_fix: In S1's done-definition, alongside 'record the current effective inherited submit type', add: 'record the current LLM-Review copyCondition value'. In S6's back-out block, add: 'revert LLM-Review copyCondition to the S1-recorded prior value and push project.config'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S2: Clarify the per-file diff fetch loop placement — either (a) specify that `assemble_merge_change_context`'s signature accepts a pre-fetched `list[tuple[filename, diff_text|None]]` and `voter.py` (or a new `gerrit_client` helper) drives the capped fetch loop before calling assemble, or (b) explicitly permit `assemble.py` to accept a `fetch_file_diff` callable parameter so the module stays import-clean.
findings.finding: The plan places `assemble_merge_change_context` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` (co-located with `DiffContext`) and simultaneously requires it to drive a per-file REST fan-out ('per-file diffs are fetched only until the combined cap is reached, remaining files listed undiffed'). The live `assemble.py` is a pure assembly module with zero REST or HTTP calls. The plan never assigns the per-file diff fetch loop — the stateful iteration that accumulates character counts and stops mid-file-list — to any specific module, leaving an ambiguous placement for the only non-trivial stateful sub-step in S2.
findings.location: S2 — 'a new `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` co-located with `DiffContext` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`' + 'per-file diffs are fetched only until the combined cap is reached'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the assemble_merge_change_context signature between the parent plan and S2 child: either update the parent plan to reflect the three-parameter form, or add a note in S2 and S4 that the child's signature supersedes the parent's and document the canonical form.
findings.finding: The parent plan documents the assembly function signature as `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` (two parameters), but the S2 child refines it to `assemble_merge_change_context(merge_files, file_diffs, mergelist)` (three parameters, per inherited finding fe192771). S4 will document the merge-back recipe and S5 will write tests that call or exercise this function; both could use the parent's two-parameter signature as their reference. The divergence is not noted in the parent plan and there is no errata or cross-reference between S2 and S4/S5 resolving which signature is canonical.
findings.location: child 29a6-59d1-a3ba-40ee (S2) — What section; parent plan S2 criterion — assemble.py paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: S2 should add an explicit note: 'The canonical signature is (merge_files, file_diffs, mergelist) — the parent plan's (auto_merge_diff, mergelist) form was a pre-refinement placeholder; S4 and S5 must use the three-parameter form.' The parent plan should also be updated on the ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a catch-up merge scenario to S5 (e.g. as part of scenario 4 or a new scenario 8): land a change on main mid-feature-development, push a reviewed `git merge --no-ff main` onto the feature branch, confirm both votes gate it, and verify the bot's auto-merge-diff context is correct for this direction of merge.
findings.finding: Catch-up merges (main→feature, described as reviewed merge changes in the Locked Design and documented via CONTRIBUTING.md in S4) are a core pattern in the design but have no S5 validation scenario. Because catch-up merges are merge commits, the bot's S2 merge-change path fires on them — but the specific behavior (auto-merge base semantics for a main→feature merge, both votes gating a catch-up merge, bot robot-comment on this case) is never exercised in the E2E test suite.
findings.location: Locked Design section ('Catch-up merges main→feature as reviewed merge changes on the branch') + S4 AC (catch-up merge recipe in CONTRIBUTING) + S5 AC (scenarios 1–7)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S4 as an explicit dependency in S5's scope/dependency section to match the parent's stated sequencing.
findings.finding: The S5 child ('Depends on S1 (config/ACLs), S2 (bot merge path), S3 (CI coverage)') does not list S4 as a dependency, yet the parent sequencing explicitly states 'S4 (after semantics locked) → S5 (last; gates epic close)' — meaning S4 must complete before S5 begins. S5's merge-back scenario (scenario 3) requires agents to follow the commit-msg hook install prerequisite and Change-Id amend recipe that S4 documents. Without S4, agents running S5 may miss the hook-install step and produce push-rejected merge commits.
findings.location: child a5b5-30db-87f9-40d3 (S5) — Scope/dependency section
findings.suggested_fix: Update S5 Scope to: 'Depends on S1 (config/ACLs), S2 (bot merge path), S3 (CI coverage), S4 (documented merge-back recipe including hook-install prerequisite).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S2: Add a fallback rule for the case where both `auto_merge_diff` is empty AND `mergelist` is zero commits — specify whether `assemble_merge_change_context` should fail-closed (return a coverage-gap result) or produce a minimal context with an explicit 'no delta and no integrated commits' notice, and update the robot-comment spec accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan defines the empty-auto-merge-diff case ('review proceeds on mergelist-only context and the robot comment states the conflict delta was empty') but is silent on the further-degenerate sub-case where the `mergelist` is also empty or the `get_mergelist` call returns zero commits. Without a specified fallback, the executor has no decision rule for this edge — fail-closed, produce a near-empty context string, or treat it as an infra error.
findings.location: S2 — 'the empty-auto-merge-diff case (clean merge) is explicitly defined — review proceeds on mergelist-only context and the robot comment states the conflict delta was empty'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S1 ADR must include an explicit placeholder section 'Cost & latency: to be populated by S6 from S5 run data' so reviewers know the section is intentionally deferred and S6's amendment is expected.
findings.finding: The amending ADR is authored and submitted as part of S1, but S6 must later add a 'Cost & latency' section sourced from S5 run data. S1's AC says 'ADR merged' with no note that the document will require a subsequent amendment by S6. Neither S1 nor S6 specifies an amendment protocol (e.g., S6 opens a new commit against the ADR file). If S1's ADR lands before S5 completes, S6 will need to re-open an already-merged document, and reviewers of S1 may approve it as 'complete' without knowing the Cost & latency section is intentionally absent.
findings.location: child fcaf-2c03-10a1-4d92 (S1) and child 72b8-4cda-ccd4-414d (S6)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Cost & latency: TBD — S6 will populate from S5 run data' placeholder section to the S1 ADR. S6's AC then becomes 'replace the placeholder with the three named metrics.' This makes the two-phase authorship visible at review time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify S3's final AC: specify that concurrency behavior is recorded as a ticket artifact (run log + ticket comment) and that S4 is responsible for any durable doc prose — OR explicitly add concurrency behavior to S4's AC list.
findings.finding: S3 child's final AC ('Concurrency behavior across branch + merge changes documented with a run example') uses 'documented' but S3's own scope statement says 'No doc prose beyond the ini spec (S4).' The AC's requirement to 'document' concurrency behavior conflicts with the self-imposed scope restriction. If 'documented' means only in a ticket comment or run log, that is not persistent documentation; if it means a doc file, it violates S3's scope boundary, which assigns prose to S4.
findings.location: child 2e8a-1a3b-66bf-4cd1 (S3) — Scope section and final AC
findings.suggested_fix: Change S3's AC to: 'Concurrency behavior across branch + merge changes verified with a run example and recorded on the ticket; S4 to incorporate into durable docs if warranted.' Add a corresponding note to S4's scope or AC so the hand-off is explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split S5 into two or three scoped validation stories: S5a (scenarios 1–3, the RED/BLUE/GREEN replay proving the core accumulation loop), S5b (scenarios 4–6, vote-carry and conflict handling), S5c (scenario 7, races, negatives, and reconciler backfill), each with its own explicit done-definition — or justify in the AC how all 7 scenarios can realistically complete within one session given CI latency.
findings.finding: S5 is likely not completable within a single working session: it specifies 7 live E2E scenarios requiring multiple CI pipeline runs per scenario (each taking 10–30 min of real wall-clock), parallel agent coordination, Gerrit ACL enforcement tests, reconciler backfill tests, and vote-carry verification — in addition to scripting and recording change URLs and votes for each scenario.
findings.location: S5 — Live multi-agent E2E validation (AC bullet + Success Criteria)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deploy-safety step for the voter.py S2 rollout: either a feature flag (`MERGE_CHANGE_REVIEW_ENABLED=true/false` env var defaulting to false, reviewed off then flipped) or a shadow-mode smoke test against a canary change before the full deploy, so a broken import or regression in the new code path can be caught and killed without affecting non-merge review traffic.
findings.finding: The voter.py deployment has no feature flag or canary stage — a single deploy flips 100% of change-review traffic to the new code, meaning a regression in the non-merge path (e.g. a broken import, an accidental behavior change in the parent-count check) would immediately affect ALL in-flight reviews with no safe partial rollout or fast-kill switch.
findings.location: S2 / Sequencing & risk section / S6 back-out
findings.suggested_fix: Add an env-var guard `MERGE_CHANGE_REVIEW_ENABLED` (default `false`) that gates entry to the new merge-detection branch in `review_and_vote`; deploy with the flag off, verify non-merge reviews are unaffected, then set the flag to `true`. Document the flag in S6's back-out section alongside the image-redeploy step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend S5 scenario 7 to include a captured rejection: a **Registered User identity** (not in `feature-branch-drivers`) attempts `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/refs/heads/main` with a merge commit and the Gerrit rejection response (`push not permitted` or `not allowed to push merge commit`) is recorded on the ticket as proof the `pushMerge` separation from the general `push = group Registered Users` grant holds on the running Gerrit 3.14.1.
findings.finding: The plan grants `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` to `feature-branch-drivers` (S1) and relies on this ACL to bound who can push merge changes targeting main. However, S1's done-definition does not include a live negative-path verification that a Registered User (who holds `push` on `refs/for/refs/heads/*`) CANNOT push a merge commit to `refs/for/main` — the S5 scenario 7 negative test ('a merge push WITHOUT pushMerge is refused') covers the no-ACL-at-all case but is scoped to the feature-branch-drivers group context. Without a captured rejection fixture from a non-feature-branch-drivers identity attempting a merge push to refs/for/main, the `pushMerge` ACL boundary is declared but not independently verified at the trust boundary.
findings.location: S1 (ACL config done-definition) and S5 scenario 7 (negatives)
findings.suggested_fix: In the S5 scenario 7 script section, add a step: create a test merge commit in a throwaway clone authenticated as a non-feature-branch-drivers service account (or the `admin` account temporarily demoted for the test), push to `refs/for/refs/heads/main`, assert the push is rejected with a Gerrit ACL error, and capture the error text as a fixture recorded on the ticket.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
